flashcards.front: What does WORA stand for in Java?
flashcards.back: Write Once Run Anywhere - Java code written on one platform can run on any platform with Java Virtual Machine without recompilation.
Java Virtual Machine - an abstract computing machine that enables Java bytecode to be executed on different platforms.

flashcards.front: What is the difference between JDK and JRE?
flashcards.back: JDK (Java Development Kit) includes development tools (compiler, debugger) for creating Java programs. JRE (Java Runtime Environment) provides runtime environment to execute Java programs.
Platform-independent intermediate code generated by Java compiler (.class file) that is executed by JVM.

flashcards.front: Name the 8 primitive data types in Java
flashcards.back: byte, short, int, long, float, double, char, boolean

flashcards.front: What is the default value of int in Java?
flashcards.back: The default value of int is 0.

flashcards.front: What is the size of int in Java?
flashcards.back: int is 32-bit signed primitive type with range from -2,147,483,648 to 2,147,483,647.
Converting one data type to another. Two types: implicit (widening) and explicit (narrowing).

flashcards.front: What are control statements in Java?
flashcards.back: Statements that control the flow of program execution: selection (if-else, switch), iteration (for, while, do-while), jump (break, continue, return).

flashcards.front: What is the difference between while and do-while loop?
flashcards.back: while loop checks condition first (may not execute), do-while executes at least once then checks condition.

flashcards.front: What is a class in Java?
flashcards.back: A blueprint/template that defines properties (fields) and behaviors (methods) for objects.

flashcards.front: What is an object in Java?
flashcards.back: An instance of a class that occupies memory and can be manipulated at runtime.

flashcards.front: What is a constructor in Java?
flashcards.back: A special method called when an object is created, used to initialize the object. Has same name as class, no return type.

flashcards.front: What are access modifiers in Java?
flashcards.back: Keywords controlling visibility: private, default (package-private), protected, public.
